title: The Scroll of Existence (A-BA-KA-RA)
author: ABASID 1841
description: The sacred teaching of A-BA-KA-RA: Existence (A), Matter (BA), Sound (KA), and Light (RA). This scroll reveals how all creation flows from pure Existence through form, vibration, and illumination.

verses.text: Before number, there was no counting.
Before motion, there was no distance.
Before sound, there was no hearing.
Before light, there was no seeing.
Yet Existence was.
Existence was not a thing among things. Existence was not greater than another. Existence did not act, for action had not yet appeared.
Existence allowed. And the name given to Existence was A.
A had no beginning, for beginning requires sequence.
A had no end, for ending requires time.
A did not move, yet movement arose within it.
A did not speak, yet sound later filled it.
A did not shine, yet light later crossed it.
A was not empty, but open. And within A rested all possibility, unformed, unspoken, unseen.
And A did not command, for command requires voice. And A did not choose, for choice requires alternatives.
A simply was.
Within the openness of A, BA arose. BA was form. BA was substance. BA was boundary.
BA could be touched. BA could be weighed. BA could rest in place.
BA gave shape to possibility. BA made 'here' different from 'there'.
But BA was silent. BA was dark. BA was unseen.
For matter without motion has no voice, and matter without light has no image.
And BA dwelt fully within A, for without Existence, matter cannot be.
Then movement stirred within BA. And where BA moved, KA was born.
KA was vibration. KA was rhythm. KA was motion passed from one form to another.
KA could not exist alone. KA required BA — air, water, stone.
KA moved from particle to particle, touching each before moving on.
And because KA must touch each form, KA traveled slowly. And when BA was still, KA ceased.
For silence is not the opposite of sound, but the rest of matter.
And from KA came IZWI — the Word, for vibration shaped meaning.
Then energy was released, and RA burst forth. RA was not vibration of matter.
RA was the release of energy itself. RA did not require BA to carry it. RA crossed emptiness freely.
RA moved as wave and pulse, yet carried no weight.
RA traveled faster than KA, for KA must push matter, but RA rides energy.
And RA revealed BA, for form becomes visible only in light.
And from RA came ZIVO — knowledge, for what is seen can be known.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER V — THE MEASURE OF SPEED
verses.text: KA moves through matter, measured in hundreds of paces per breath.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER V — THE MEASURE OF SPEED
verses.text: RA moves through space, measured in hundreds of millions of paces in a heartbeat.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER V — THE MEASURE OF SPEED
verses.text: Therefore sound arrives after light. Therefore thunder follows lightning.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER V — THE MEASURE OF SPEED
verses.text: And the children learned: Slowness is not weakness, and speed is not greatness. Each serves its place within Existence.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VI — THE ORDER OF DEPENDENCE
verses.text: A depends on nothing. BA depends on A. KA depends on BA. RA depends on energy, not matter.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VI — THE ORDER OF DEPENDENCE
verses.text: Thus all are different, yet none are enemies.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VI — THE ORDER OF DEPENDENCE
verses.text: Existence allows. Matter forms. Sound speaks. Light reveals.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VII — THE FIGURES OF UNDERSTANDING
verses.text: And the children saw them as figures: A, the Silent Ground. BA, the Bearer of Form. KA, the Dancing Voice. RA, the Shining Revealer.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VII — THE FIGURES OF UNDERSTANDING
verses.text: They were not gods competing, but principles cooperating. And none claimed to be A, for Existence cannot be acted.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VIII — THE EMBODIMENT OF RA
verses.text: And in the fullness of matter, RA sought expression. Not to become Existence, but to appear within it.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VIII — THE EMBODIMENT OF RA
verses.text: And RA found embodiment in form, and the embodiment was called ABASID 1841.

verses.chapter: CHAPTER VIII — THE EMBODIMENT OF RA
verses.text: Not as the source of light, but as its witness and carrier. For light shines through form, but Existence alone allows all.
Existence does not act. Action appears within Existence.
Light does not create Being. Being allows light.
Sound does not exist alone. Matter gives it voice.
And nothing stands above Existence, for 'above' requires position.
Thus the children were taught: A — to be. BA — to have form. KA — to move and speak. RA — to reveal and know.
And Existence was not a thing, yet nothing could be without it.
And this teaching was named: A – BA – KA – RA.
